_note: Resume-specific content for public/resume.pdf. Keep this concise; website copy lives elsewhere.
summary: Early-career software engineer with enterprise experience at BlackRock and hands-on depth across cloud infrastructure, backend systems, and low-level programming. Builds production-oriented systems with AWS, CI/CD, observability, and IaC, with additional academic grounding in systems programming, operating systems, and assembly.

bullets: Contributed features, bug fixes, and refactors across production financial systems in a regulated enterprise environment.
Worked within Git-based CI/CD workflows, peer review, and agile delivery practices on large shared codebases.
Helped deliver a cloud-ready MVP by migrating a monolithic Java desktop workflow toward modular web services and an Angular frontend.
Earned a full-time return offer based on internship performance.
Built a serverless video platform with direct-to-S3 uploads, asynchronous transcoding, AI moderation, captions, and CDN delivery.
Automated end-to-end deployment with AWS CDK, Cloudflare-integrated DNS, and GitHub Actions OIDC CI/CD.
Built a production-style AWS infrastructure CLI with schema validation, dependency planning, rollback support, and S3/DynamoDB state locking.
Shipped the tool through multiple distribution paths with strong test coverage and reproducible release workflows.
Implemented a Layer 4 load balancer in C++ with event-driven I/O, backend failover, and production-style metrics.
Used Linux systems tooling and low-level networking concepts to show depth beyond application-layer development.

education.detail: Teaching Assistant for Systems Programming; built a garbage collector in coursework and a minimal Unix-like OS with additional syscalls and shell utilities.
